title: Kim2025 – 40-class beta-range SSVEP speller dataset
description: A 40-class steady-state visually evoked potential (SSVEP) brain-computer interface dataset designed to reduce visual fatigue through beta-range frequency stimulation (14.0–21.8 Hz). The dataset comprises 33-channel EEG recordings from 40 healthy participants performing a speller task across 6 sessions, with stimuli presented using the joint frequency-phase modulation (JFPM) approach. This resource supports the development and benchmarking of low-fatigue BCI applications.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired at 1024 Hz using a BioSemi ActiveTwo system with 31 scalp electrodes plus 2 mastoid references (standard 10-05 montage). Participants completed 6 blocks of 40 trials per session, with each trial consisting of 1.5 s rest, 0.5 s cue, and 5.0 s SSVEP stimulation. Stimuli were presented in a 5×8 matrix on a 120 Hz monitor using JFPM with 0.5π phase differences between adjacent stimuli. Stored epochs span [−2000, 5000] ms relative to stimulus onset.
